---
title: "LOLA histone-mark enrichment on preclinical differential methylation"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Uses the LOLA enrichment object produced by RnBeads differential
# methylation (01_rnbeads_hpv.R). Summarises H3K27me3, H3K9me3 and
# H3K4me3 signals between HPV+/HPV- and overlays leading-edge genes
# derived from the GSEA pipeline.
source(here::here("config.R"))
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(DESeq2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# LOLA enrichment object produced during differential methylation analysis.
load(file.path(PRECLIN_METH_OUT,
               "differential_methylation_data",
               "differential_rnbDiffMeth",
               "enrichment_lola.RData"))

lola_df <- as.data.frame(
  diffmeth.lola.enrichment$region[["HPV_Positive vs. HPV_Negative (based on Untreated)"]]$genes
)
#write.csv(lola_df, file.path(PRECLIN_METH_OUT, "lola_untreated.csv"))
```


```{r}
# Select relevant histone marks (H3K27me3, H3K9me3, H3K4me3)
histone_marks <- c("H3K27me3", "H3K9me3", "H3K4me3")

# Filter LOLA results to keep only selected histone modifications
histone_lola <- lola_df %>%
  filter(antibody %in% histone_marks)

# Use `userSet` to classify **hyper** (HPV+) vs **hypo** (HPV-)
histone_lola <- histone_lola %>%
  mutate(HPV_Group = ifelse(grepl("hyper", userSet), "HPV_Positive", "HPV_Negative"))
```


```{r}
histone_lola <- histone_lola %>%
  mutate(qValue = ifelse(qValue > 0.99, 0.99, qValue),   # Cap large qValues to avoid log(1)
         qValue = ifelse(qValue < 1e-10, 1e-10, qValue), # Avoid log(0) issues
         weighted_score = oddsRatio * qValue)            # Compute weighted score

histone_summary <- histone_lola %>%
  group_by(HPV_Group, antibody) %>%
  summarise(weighted_histone_score = median(weighted_score, na.rm = TRUE))

print(histone_summary)
```


```{r}
# Reshape histone summary to wide format
histone_wide <- histone_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = HPV_Group, values_from = weighted_histone_score)

# Initialize results dataframe
histone_results <- data.frame(Histone_Mark = histone_wide$antibody, P_Value = NA, Test_Type = "Wilcoxon")

# Loop over histone modifications and apply Wilcoxon test
for (i in 1:nrow(histone_wide)) {
  data_pos <- histone_wide$HPV_Positive[i]
  data_neg <- histone_wide$HPV_Negative[i]

  # Perform Wilcoxon test (since we only have summary values)
  test_result <- wilcox.test(x = data_pos, y = data_neg,
                             alternative = "two.sided", exact = FALSE)

  # Store p-value
  histone_results$P_Value[i] <- test_result$p.value
}

# Adjust for multiple testing (FDR correction)
histone_results$FDR <- p.adjust(histone_results$P_Value, method = "BH")

# View significant histone modifications (FDR < 0.05)
significant_histones <- histone_results %>% filter(FDR < 0.05)

print(histone_results)
print(significant_histones)
```


# Leading-edge gene overlay
```{r}
### Filter counts for leading-edge genes and apply VST.
sig <- read.csv(file.path(PRECLIN_TX_DATA, "countall.csv"))
sig = aggregate(sig, FUN = max, by = list(sig$Genes))
sig <- sig[,-1]
rownames(sig) <- sig$Genes
sig <- sig[,-1]
sig <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(object = as.matrix(sig))
```

```{r}
# Define leading edge genes
leadingedge <- c("EREG", "MEI1", "RIBC2", "TCAM1P", "SMC1B", "LY75", "SP9", "ZSCAN23")

# Filter sig matrix for leading edge genes
leadingedge_data <- sig[rownames(sig) %in% leadingedge, ]
```

```{r}
# Define HPV+ and HPV- sample indices
hpv_neg_samples <- 1:9   # First 9 columns are HPV-negative
hpv_pos_samples <- 10:18 # Next 9 columns are HPV-positive

# Convert leading edge gene expression to long format
gene_long <- data.frame(
  Name         = rownames(leadingedge_data),
  HPV_Negative = rowMeans(leadingedge_data[, hpv_neg_samples], na.rm = TRUE),
  HPV_Positive = rowMeans(leadingedge_data[, hpv_pos_samples], na.rm = TRUE),
  Type         = "Gene"
)
print(head(gene_long))
```

```{r}
# Convert histone summary to long format
histone_long <- histone_summary %>%
  rename(Name = antibody) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = HPV_Group, values_from = weighted_histone_score) %>%
  mutate(Type = "Histone")

print(head(histone_long))
```

```{r}
# Combine genes and histones in a single dataframe
combined_data <- bind_rows(gene_long, histone_long)
print(head(combined_data))
```
